name: AI Customer Support V2
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Conversational AI engine — customer query understanding, sentiment-aware response generation, multi-turn dialogue management, knowledge-grounded answers, escalation decision-making, and automatic ticket classification using GPT-4o for complex cases and GPT-4o-mini for routine inquiries

dev.note: ~80K tokens/day, chatbot prototyping with sample conversations

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Knowledge base retrieval — semantic search across product documentation, FAQ libraries, troubleshooting guides, past resolved tickets, and internal SOPs for grounded response generation with citation support

services.name: Azure Communication Services
services.purpose: Omnichannel customer engagement — live chat widget, email integration, SMS notifications for ticket updates, voice call routing for escalation, and video support for complex troubleshooting scenarios

enterprise.note: ~100K chat sessions, ~20K SMS, ~5K voice minutes, video

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Conversation and ticket state management — multi-turn conversation history, customer profiles, ticket lifecycle tracking, sentiment trend data, escalation records, SLA timers, and agent performance analytics

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Support platform runtime — hosts the AI chatbot service, sentiment analysis pipeline, escalation engine, ticket routing logic, and integration APIs for CRM and ticketing systems

services.name: Azure Service Bus
services.purpose: Asynchronous message routing — escalation queue for human agent handoff, ticket creation events, CRM sync messages, notification triggers, and dead-letter handling for failed processing

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, Communication Services connection strings, CRM integration credentials, search service keys, and customer data encryption keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Support platform observability — conversation resolution rate, sentiment trends, escalation rates, response latency, customer satisfaction scores (CSAT), and SLA compliance tracking

optimization_tips: Route routine inquiries (password reset, order status, FAQ-type questions) to GPT-4o-mini and reserve GPT-4o for complex troubleshooting, complaint handling, and multi-step issue resolution — reduces AI costs by 50-65%

Implement conversation-level semantic caching — many customers ask the same questions; cache responses for common queries (shipping policy, return process, pricing) with 30-minute TTL, reducing redundant inference by 25-35%
Use AI Search to ground responses in product documentation before calling OpenAI — providing relevant context in the prompt reduces hallucination and follow-up clarification tokens by 30-40%

Implement intent classification as the first AI call using GPT-4o-mini — classify the query intent (billing, technical, general, complaint) in a single cheap call, then route to specialized prompts with targeted context, avoiding loading all knowledge for every query

Configure auto-escalation rules based on sentiment thresholds — if customer sentiment drops below 0.3 for 3+ messages, escalate to human agent immediately instead of continuing AI loops that consume tokens without resolving the issue
Use Communication Services chat over voice for first-contact resolution — chat interactions cost ~$0.004 vs voice at ~$0.04/minute, and AI can handle text more efficiently than transcribed speech
